title: Cannot sync filter query params with the searchState, rerouting issues.
body:
## 🐛 Bug description

<!-- A clear and concise description of what the bug is. -->

### There are 2 bugs with react instant search at the moment that impact user experience and almost make it unusable.

**1.** When routing back to the search page the scrolling position restores(meaning that if you have potentially scroll all the way down to where you left off)
**2.** When trying to sync the url with searchstate, clicking on a **RefinementList** components checkbox 

## 🔍 Bug reproduction

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
To reproduce bug algolia/react-instantsearch#1

1. Scroll down few results down
3. Click on button "Reroute"
4. After rerouting to another page click on a button "Go back"
5. You will be back on the top of the page and not where you left off.

To reproduce bug algolia/react-instantsearch#2

1. On the search page click on any filter checkbox
3. You will see that it's not active
4. click it multiple times
5. nothing happens but url gets bloated (https://zk8t84.sse.codesandbox.io/?query=&page=1&refinementList%5Bbrand%5D%5B0%5D%5B0%5D%5B0%5D=HP&refinementList%5Bbrand%5D%5B...)

<!-- A live example helps a lot! Fork the sandbox, reproduce the bug and paste the URL here: -->

**Live reproduction:**

https://codesandbox.io/s/funny-night-zk8t84

## 💭 Expected behavior

<!-- A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen. -->

Expectation is quite simple, when going back from detail pge to search results, I want to go back to where I left off, not back at the top.

I also want to be able to use filters and sync them to my search state.

## 🖥 Screenshots
![Screenshot 2022-04-15 at 17 38 07](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20464922/163596998-eca594ff-1266-4ffe-8734-87134ce58d45.png)

<!-- If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem. -->

## Environment

- OS: [macOS]
- Browser: [Chrome]
- Version: 100.0.4896.75 (Official Build) (arm64)

## Additional context

I beg the team to fix these bugs, as it will help so many people using websites built with react-instantsearch, looking forward to hearing from you.

labels.description: Issues in any of the react-instantsearch@6 packages
labels.name: Library: React InstantSearch < 7
